247 unread emails, and the only thing I want to know is whether the waves are good there today.

items.sourceNote: 「travel addict on the road to recovery, just kidding heading to the airport」 register，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
我正在努力戒掉旅行成癮，
目前進度良好——

你看，
我現在就很冷靜地
坐在前往機場的客運上。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合搶到票後的 IG 限動、半夜訂完機票的 Threads 自嘲、跟朋友坦白又要出國的 LINE 訊息——把「戒不掉」 翻轉成段子，自帶反差笑點
variations: 我戒旅行的決心非常堅定，堅定到下一班機已經劃好位了。
別擔心，我已經控制住了，這趟只是去機場散散步，順便登機而已。

en.content:
I'm working hard on quitting my travel addiction,
and progress is great —

see,
right now I'm very calmly
sitting on the bus to the airport.
en.editorNote: For the just-booked-it IG story, the 2 AM 'I did it again' Threads post, or the 'I'm leaving again' confession to friends — flips the 'can't quit' line into a self-aware punchline
variations: My resolve to quit traveling is rock solid — solid enough that my next seat is already assigned.
Don't worry, I've got it under control. This trip is just a little walk to the airport. With boarding.

items.sourceNote: 「budget trip then adopts an alpaca」 over-spending register，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
我這趟的預算規劃非常嚴謹：

結果我點了高級調酒、
報名了跳傘、
幫舅舅的新女友買了伴手禮、
還差一點認養了一隻草泥馬。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合度假帳單爆炸後的 IG 限動、Threads 懺悔長文、回國盤點戰利品的廢發——把「預算崩盤」的荒謬升級，誇張到讓人會心一笑
variations: 我的旅遊預算是用來欣賞的，不是用來遵守的。
出發前精打細算，到了當地我連海風都想打賞。

en.content:
My budget plan for this trip was extremely strict.

Then I ordered the top-shelf cocktail,
signed up for skydiving,
bought a souvenir for my uncle's new girlfriend,
and very nearly adopted an alpaca.
en.editorNote: For the blown-budget IG story, the Threads confession thread, or the post-trip 'how did this happen' haul post — pushes the over-spending bit to absurd heights for a knowing laugh
variations: My travel budget is there to be admired, not obeyed.
Penny-pinching before the trip; tipping the sea breeze once I land.

items.sourceNote: 「airplane mode: not just for phones」 register，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
飛航模式
從來都不只是手機的功能，
它也是我大腦
登機那一刻
自動開啟的設定。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合起飛前關機自拍、機艙窗景照、Threads 出國放空宣言——把「飛航模式」 一語雙關用在大腦放空上，輕巧又有梗
variations: 起飛的那一刻，我把手機、工作群組、跟我的責任感一起調成飛航模式。
飛航模式最棒的功能不是省電，是讓那些找我的人暫時找不到我。

en.content:
Airplane mode
was never just a phone setting.
It's also what my brain
switches to automatically
the second I board.
en.editorNote: For the powering-down-before-takeoff selfie, the window-view cabin shot, or the 'going offline' Threads declaration — a light pun that turns airplane mode into a state of mind
variations: The moment we take off, I set my phone, my group chats, and my sense of responsibility all to airplane mode.
The best feature of airplane mode isn't saving battery. It's that nobody who needs me can find me.

items.sourceNote: 「tans fade, tan lines tell stories」 register，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
曬黑會退，
但這條曬痕
是我這禮拜唯一
認真完成的事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合海邊度假回來的 IG 限動、曬痕特寫、Threads 自嘲——把「曬傷」這件小事升格成度假的勳章，自帶笑點又有畫面
variations: 別人帶回紀念品，我帶回一條夾腳拖形狀的曬痕。
曬黑會退，但那幾天什麼都不想的快樂，希望可以留久一點。

en.content:
The tan will fade,
but this tan line
is the only thing
I fully committed to all week.
en.editorNote: For the post-beach selfie, the flip-flop-tan-line close-up, or a Threads vacation brag — turns a sunburn into a badge of honor with a wink
variations: Other people bring back souvenirs. I brought back a tan line shaped like a sandal.
The tan fades, but I'm hoping the not-thinking-about-anything part sticks around a little longer.

items.sourceNote: 「hotel breakfast buffet as therapy」 register，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
我不需要心理諮商，
我只需要一間
附自助早餐
而且可以裝第三盤
沒人會看我的飯店。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合飯店自助餐盤特寫、滿桌早餐平拍、Threads 度假廢發——把「飯店吃到飽」 的療癒感講到位，每個愛裝第三盤的人都會點頭
variations: 人生問題的解法，通常藏在飯店早餐的第二輪可頌裡。
我度假的高峰不是看日出，是早餐還沒收、我還能再去夾一盤。

en.content:
I don't need therapy.
I need a hotel
with a breakfast buffet
where nobody judges me
for going back for a third plate.
en.editorNote: For the loaded-buffet-plate photo, the morning-spread flat lay, or a Threads vacation post — nails the quiet euphoria of unlimited hotel breakfast
variations: Most of my problems get solved somewhere around the second croissant at the hotel breakfast.
The peak of my vacation isn't the sunrise. It's realizing the buffet's still open and I can go again.

items.sourceNote: 「got sunburned, that's how I travel」 frame，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
我擦了 SPF50、
躲在陽傘下、
還穿了長袖。

結果還是曬傷了。

我的皮膚不是白，
是出廠就沒裝防曬功能。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合曬傷脫皮的崩潰自拍、海邊回來的 Threads 哀號、防曬戰利品開箱——把白到曬傷的體質寫成一個自嘲到底的段子
variations: 我去海邊的成果，是一份沙子三明治跟一身脫皮。
防曬係數我是買到頂了，可惜我的皮膚不收這套。

en.content:
I wore SPF 50,
hid under an umbrella,
and kept my sleeves on.

Still got sunburned.

My skin didn't ship with sun protection installed.
en.editorNote: For the peeling-shoulders selfie, the post-beach Threads lament, or the sunscreen-haul photo — turns burning-in-the-shade pale skin into a self-roast
variations: My beach haul: one sandy sandwich and a full-body peel.
I maxed out the SPF. My skin just doesn't accept the warranty.

items.sourceNote: 「every trip starts with optimism, ends with laundry」 frame，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
每趟旅行都是
從滿懷期待開始，
以一籃洗不完的衣服結束。

中間那段叫人生，
兩頭那段叫現實。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合回國後堆成山的待洗衣物照、Threads 收假廢發、旅行回顧文結尾——把旅行頭尾的反差寫得既好笑又有點哲學
variations: 旅行的開始是登機證，結束是洗衣機的嗶嗶聲。
出發時我什麼都帶了，回來時只想把全部丟進洗衣籃假裝沒事。

en.content:
Every trip
starts with optimism
and ends with laundry.

The part in the middle is the life.
The parts on either end are the reality.
en.editorNote: For the mountain-of-dirty-clothes photo, the back-from-vacation Threads post, or the closing line of a trip recap — captures the bookends of travel with a wry, slightly philosophical twist
variations: Travel begins with a boarding pass and ends with the beep of a washing machine.
I packed everything for the trip. Coming home, I just want to throw it all in the hamper and pretend it didn't happen.

items.sourceNote: 「souvenirs because my suitcase wasn't full enough」 frame，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
我買紀念品的理由很簡單：

回程的行李箱
還空著一個拳頭的位置，
那是對旅行的不尊重。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合戰利品平拍、伴手禮開箱、回程行李硬塞的 Threads 廢發——把買到失控的紀念品癮，講成一種使命感
variations: 我不是亂買，我是替未來那個會後悔的自己先做好回憶準備。
紀念品的功能不是好看，是把空蕩蕩的行李箱填滿到拉鍊哀號。

en.content:
My reason for buying souvenirs is simple:

there was still a fist-sized gap
in my suitcase on the way home,
and that's just disrespectful to the trip.
en.editorNote: For the souvenir-haul flat lay, the gift-box unboxing, or the 'cramming the return suitcase' Threads post — reframes compulsive souvenir buying as a sacred duty
variations: I'm not overbuying. I'm pre-loading memories for the future me who'll regret it.
Souvenirs aren't about looking nice. They're about filling an empty suitcase until the zipper screams.

items.sourceNote: 「passport wanted more stamps of approval」雙關 frame，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
我的護照
比我還努力爭取肯定，

它一年蓋的章，
比我一年得到的稱讚
還多。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合護照內頁特寫、出入境章蓋滿的炫耀照、Threads 自嘲短發——把蓋章的成就感跟現實生活的肯定荒涼感對比，又好笑又有點酸
variations: 別人靠 KPI 證明自己，我靠護照頁數。
我這輩子收集最認真的兩樣東西：護照上的章，跟沒人按讚的回憶。

en.content:
My passport
works harder for approval than I do.

It collects more stamps in a year
than I collect compliments.
en.editorNote: For the passport-pages close-up, the stacked-stamps brag shot, or the self-deprecating Threads post — contrasts the thrill of stamps with real-life validation droughts, funny with a touch of bittersweet
variations: Other people prove themselves with KPIs. I prove mine with passport pages.
The two things I've collected most seriously in life: passport stamps and memories nobody liked.

items.sourceNote: 「my soul's still on vacation, body's back to reality」 frame，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
收假第一天，
我的身體準時打卡上班，

但我的靈魂
還躺在那片海邊，
說它沒收到通知。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合收假第一天的辦公室自拍、Threads 連假後哀號、IG 限動廢發——把「人到了心沒到」的收假症候群寫得超有畫面
variations: 我人在會議室，魂在海關還沒過。
收假不是回到現實，是被現實強制登出度假模式。

en.content:
First day back from vacation:
my body clocked in on time,

but my soul
is still lying on that beach,
claiming it never got the memo.
en.editorNote: For the back-to-the-office selfie, the post-long-weekend Threads lament, or the Monday-morning IG story — nails the 'body present, mind absent' return-to-work syndrome
variations: I'm physically in the meeting room. Spiritually I'm still stuck at customs.
Coming back isn't returning to reality. It's reality force-logging you out of vacation mode.

items.sourceNote: 「what did the beach say? long time no sea」雙關 frame，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
好久不見大海，
它一句話都沒說，

只是用一波又一波的浪
把我這一年的心累
全部沖回外海。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合海邊放空照、踏浪自拍、Threads 海邊療癒短發——保留「long time no sea」的海邊重逢感，但走療癒路線，比純諧音更有溫度
variations: 我跟海說了好多話，它一句也沒回，卻好像全聽懂了。
海不負責給答案，它只負責把問題一波波帶走。

en.content:
Long time, no sea.

The ocean didn't say a word back —
it just pulled a whole year of tiredness
out of me,
one wave at a time.
en.editorNote: For the staring-at-the-horizon shot, the toes-in-the-water selfie, or the seaside-reset Threads post — keeps the 'long time no sea' reunion pun but turns it gentle and restorative
variations: I told the sea everything. It didn't answer once, but somehow it heard all of it.
The ocean doesn't give you answers. It just carries the questions away, wave by wave.

items.sourceNote: 「all I need is a passport and some snacks」 frame，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
出國我只需要三樣東西：
護照、
一點零食、
還有一個騙自己「這次絕對不會亂花錢」的信念。

前兩樣最後都用完了，
第三樣從來沒實現過。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合登機前的隨身包平拍、機場零食特寫、Threads 出國前廢發——用三段式遞進把旅行的天真期待跟現實落差講得很好笑
variations: 旅行必備清單：護照、行動電源、跟一個註定破功的省錢計畫。
我帶的零食撐不過第一段航程，我的預算撐不過第一天。

en.content:
All I need for a trip is three things:
a passport,
some snacks,
and the firm belief that this time I won't overspend.

The first two run out.
The third one never comes true.
en.editorNote: For the carry-on flat lay, the airport-snack close-up, or the pre-trip Threads post — uses a three-beat build to land the gap between travel optimism and reality
variations: Travel essentials: passport, power bank, and a budget plan that's doomed from takeoff.
My snacks don't survive the first flight. My budget doesn't survive day one.

items.sourceNote: jet lag = time travel with bad customer service frame，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
時差其實就是時空旅行，
只是售後服務特別差：
沒有客服可以打、
沒有退費可以申請、
你只能在凌晨三點清醒地接受它。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合回國後黑眼圈自拍、凌晨清醒滑手機的 Threads 廢發、IG 限動——把時差用「時空旅行」的比喻包裝，比單純喊累有梗
variations: 時差是身體版的當機，重開機要三天，期間不接受任何客訴。
我不是失眠，我是還卡在上一個時區的客服等待室裡。

en.content:
Jet lag is just time travel
with really bad customer service:
no hotline to call,
no refund to request,
just you, wide awake at 3 AM, accepting your fate.
en.editorNote: For the under-eye-circles selfie, the 3 AM scroll, or the first-day-back Threads post — wraps the tired cliché in a 'time travel' bit that lands better than just complaining
variations: Jet lag is your body crashing. Reboot takes three days and the support line is closed.
I'm not sleepless. I'm still stuck in the previous time zone's waiting room.

items.sourceNote: layover / 轉機 register，原創

zh-tw.content:
轉機等待是一種特殊的時間狀態：
不算在出發地，
也不算在目的地，
你就懸在一個全世界都在趕路、
只有你不知道現在幾點的航廈裡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合轉機航廈的空景照、登機門前發呆自拍、Threads 半夜轉機廢發——把「卡在中間」那種懸浮感寫到位，長途旅人特別有共鳴
variations: 轉機八小時，我認識了三家免稅店、兩個充電座、跟一個徹底放空的自己。
轉機時間不屬於昨天也不屬於今天，它屬於一個叫做「待會兒」的平行宇宙。

en.content:
A layover is its own kind of time:
not quite where you left,
not quite where you're going,
just you suspended in a terminal
where everyone's rushing and you've lost track of the hour.
en.editorNote: For the empty-terminal photo, the staring-into-space gate selfie, or the late-night Threads post — captures that limbo feeling every long-haul traveler knows
variations: Eight-hour layover: I met three duty-free shops, two charging stations, and one completely zoned-out version of myself.
Layover time doesn't belong to yesterday or today. It belongs to a parallel universe called 'in a bit.'

items.sourceNote: window seat = empty bank account frame，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
我永遠選靠窗的位子，
因為那扇窗
跟我訂完機票後的存款一樣，
空空的，
但風景真的很美。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合機艙窗景照、雲海特寫、刷卡買票後的 Threads 自嘲——把「靠窗」跟「破產」做雙關，比單純拍雲有故事
variations: 靠窗的好處是：看著外面的雲，就比較不會去想戶頭裡剩下的數字。
我選靠窗不是為了風景，是為了有個地方可以靠著消化帳單的衝擊。

en.content:
I always pick the window seat,
because that window
is just like my bank account
after booking the flight:
empty, but the view is gorgeous.
en.editorNote: For the cloud-sea window shot or the post-booking Threads confession — turns the classic 'window seat = broke' one-liner into something with a little more ache and charm
variations: The upside of the window seat: staring at clouds means not staring at my balance.
I didn't choose the window for the view. I chose it for something to lean on while the bill sinks in.

items.sourceNote: travel insurance / 旅平險 register，原創

zh-tw.content:
出國前買旅平險的心情很微妙：
你花錢，
是為了希望這筆錢
永遠用不到。

這大概是人生中
唯一一次
真心希望自己虧本的消費。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合出國前手機投保截圖、登機前 Threads 廢發、IG 出國準備系列——把買保險這件無聊事寫出哲學感跟笑點
variations: 旅平險是我唯一一筆「希望它白花」的開銷。
我買旅平險的邏輯：花小錢買一個「拜託不要用到」的安心。

en.content:
Buying travel insurance is a strange feeling:
you pay money
hoping you'll never have to use it.

It might be the only purchase in life
where you genuinely root
for yourself to lose money.
en.editorNote: For the in-app insurance screenshot, the pre-trip Threads post, or the 'getting ready' carousel — turns a boring errand into a small philosophical joke
variations: Travel insurance is the one expense I actively hope was a waste of money.
My insurance logic: pay a little for the peace of 'please, please don't let me need this.'

items.sourceNote: souvenir = suitcase wasn't full enough frame，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
我買紀念品的理由很單純：
因為我的行李箱
還沒有滿到
讓我後悔的程度。

現在滿了，
我後悔了，
但我快樂。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合戰利品開箱平拍、回程行李超重的 Threads 廢發、IG 紀念品系列——把「明知會後悔還是買」的旅人通病寫成輕喜劇
variations: 紀念品的意義不在於有沒有用，在於它證明了我那一刻真的很想擁有快樂。
我帶回來的不是紀念品，是一整箱「當下覺得很值得」的證據。

en.content:
My reason for buying souvenirs is simple:
my suitcase
wasn't quite full enough
to make me regret it yet.

Now it is.
I regret it.
I'm happy.
en.editorNote: For the souvenir-haul flat lay, the overweight-bag Threads post, or the 'why did I buy this' IG carousel — turns the buy-it-anyway impulse into a tiny three-beat comedy
variations: Souvenirs aren't about usefulness. They're proof that, just then, I really wanted to hold onto happiness.
I didn't bring back souvenirs. I brought back a suitcase full of 'it felt worth it at the time.'

items.sourceNote: 2026 暑假 61 天 timely register，原創

zh-tw.content:
暑假有六十一天，
我規劃了十二趟旅行、
存了八個景點、
收藏了三十篇攻略。

目前實際出門的次數：
零。
zh-tw.editorNote: 2026 暑假 7/1–8/30 主場期最 timely 的廢文 register；適合放暑假計畫拖延的 Threads 短發、IG 限動自嘲、LINE 群組丟給只會收藏不會出門的朋友
variations: 我的暑假不是用來旅行的，是用來規劃旅行的，這兩件事差了一整個夏天。
六十一天的暑假，我花了六十天決定要去哪，最後一天決定明年再說。

en.content:
Summer break is sixty-one days long.
I planned twelve trips,
saved eight destinations,
bookmarked thirty guides.

Trips actually taken:
zero.
en.editorNote: For the summer-planning-procrastination Threads post, the bookmark-folder screenshot, or the 'all saved, none booked' IG story — names the universal gap between planning a trip and taking one
variations: My summer isn't for traveling. It's for planning to travel. Those are a whole season apart.
Sixty-one days of summer: sixty deciding where to go, one deciding to figure it out next year.

items.sourceNote: 「travel = old word for trouble/struggle」 etymology frame，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
「旅行」這個詞的古字根，
據說源自「辛苦」跟「折磨」。

所以你的班機誤點四小時，
嚴格來說，
只是回歸初衷。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合誤點電子看板特寫、登機門前的厭世自拍、Threads 等飛廢發——用冷知識把誤點的怒氣轉成段子，比直接罵航空公司有風度
variations: 誤點不是意外，是這個詞最忠於原意的時刻。
我不是在受苦，我只是在體驗「旅行」這個字最原始的版本。

en.content:
The old root of the word 'travel'
supposedly comes from 'trouble' and 'struggle.'

So your four-hour delay
is, technically,
just the word staying true to itself.
en.editorNote: For the delay-board close-up, the dead-inside gate selfie, or the Threads post mid-wait — turns delay rage into a punchline with a side of etymology, classier than yelling at the airline
variations: A delay isn't a malfunction. It's the word 'travel' being painfully honest about its origins.
I'm not suffering. I'm just experiencing the original, unedited version of the word 'travel.'

items.sourceNote: 「airplane mode / 飛航模式斷線」 register，原創

zh-tw.content:
飛機起飛、開啟飛航模式那一刻，
是現代人唯一
名正言順失聯的時刻。

沒人能找到你，
你也找不到 Wi-Fi，
大家都很安全。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合機艙窗戶雲海照、手機飛航模式截圖、Threads 出國斷線宣言——把「被迫離線」 講成一種奢侈的自由，社畜共鳴度爆表
variations: 飛航模式是地表上最便宜的數位排毒，附贈一段沒人能打擾你的雲端時光。
起飛的兩小時裡，我不是沒訊號，我是終於有藉口不回訊息。

en.content:
The moment the plane takes off
and airplane mode kicks in
is the only time a modern person
gets to be legitimately unreachable.

Nobody can find you.
You can't find Wi-Fi.
Everyone's safe.
en.editorNote: For the cloud-sea window shot, the airplane-mode screenshot, or the 'going offline' Threads declaration — reframes forced disconnection as a rare luxury every burned-out worker craves
variations: Airplane mode is the cheapest digital detox on earth, with a free stretch of cloud time nobody can interrupt.
For two hours after takeoff, I don't have no signal. I finally have an excuse not to reply.

items.sourceNote: 台東熱氣球嘉年華 2026 暑假 timely register，原創

zh-tw.content:
在台東看熱氣球升空那一刻，
我才懂了一件事：

會飛的東西，
通常都得先承受
一段被火烤、被風吹、
緩慢膨脹的等待。
zh-tw.editorNote: 2026 台東鹿野熱氣球嘉年華 timely 的在地金句——適合熱氣球升空照、光雕音樂會夜景、Threads 暑假旅行抒情短發；把景點打卡升級成有溫度的勵志語錄
variations: 熱氣球教我的事：想升高，不是用力跳，是先學會慢慢被填滿。
那顆球飛得很美，但沒人看見它被火烤過、被反覆檢查過的那些早晨。

en.content:
Watching the hot-air balloons rise in Taitung,
I finally understood something:

the things that get to fly
usually have to survive
a long, slow wait
of being heated, blown around,
and filled up bit by bit.
en.editorNote: Timely for Taiwan's 2026 Taitung hot-air balloon festival — works on the balloon-lift-off shot, the night light show, or a summer-trip Threads reflection. Turns a check-in spot into a quietly motivational quote
variations: What the hot-air balloon taught me: rising isn't about jumping harder. It's about learning to be filled slowly.
The balloon flew beautifully. Nobody saw the mornings it spent being heated, checked, and doubted.

items.sourceNote: 「catch flights not feelings」 經典 frame，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
他們說：要追的是航班，
不是感情。

結果我兩個都沒追到，
航班誤點，
感情已讀。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合登機門等待的厭世自拍、單身出遊的 Threads 自嘲、IG 旅行系列廢文——把網路名言反轉成更真實的版本，自帶笑點又有點酸
variations: 追航班、不追感情，這句話的前提是你至少要追得到其中一個。
我這趟旅行很成功，成功地把工作、感情、跟回程班機全部都錯過了。

en.content:
They say: catch flights, not feelings.

I caught neither.
The flight got delayed,
the feelings got left on read.
en.editorNote: For the world-weary gate selfie, the solo-trip Threads post, or the IG travel series — flips the viral one-liner into something more honest, funny with a little sting
variations: 'Catch flights, not feelings' assumes you can actually catch at least one of them.
This trip was a success. I successfully missed work, the feelings, and my return flight.

items.sourceNote: solo travel + free breakfast register，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
一個人旅行的好處很多，
但最被低估的一項是：

飯店早餐
沒有人跟我搶最後一塊
培根。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合飯店自助早餐特寫、一個人餐桌平拍、Threads 獨旅短發——把獨旅的孤獨感反轉成最實際的小確幸，誰看了都會笑出來
variations: 一個人吃早餐最大的自由，是想夾幾塊培根就夾幾塊，沒人會用眼神審判我。
獨旅治好了我的很多焦慮，但治不好我在自助吧第三次回去夾培根的羞恥心。

en.content:
Solo travel has a lot of perks,
but the most underrated one is this:

nobody fights me
for the last piece of bacon
at the hotel breakfast.
en.editorNote: For the hotel-buffet close-up, the table-for-one flat lay, or the solo-trip Threads post — flips the supposed loneliness of solo travel into the most practical tiny joy
variations: The real freedom of eating breakfast alone is taking as much bacon as I want, with nobody judging me with their eyes.
Solo travel cured a lot of my anxieties. It did not cure the shame of going back to the buffet for bacon a third time.

items.sourceNote: 2026 暑假 61 天 + 人潮 timely register，原創

zh-tw.content:
暑假出去玩最大的覺悟是：

你想逃離的人群，
剛好也都
買了跟你同一天
同一個景點的票。
zh-tw.editorNote: 2026 暑假 7/1–8/30 主場期最 timely 的廢文 register；適合放熱門景點排隊照、人擠人海邊照、Threads 暑假出遊哀號——把暑假旅遊最痛的人潮一刀切中
variations: 暑假旅行教我的事：你以為的祕境，是全台灣兩千三百萬人共同的祕密。
我特地避開上班日出遊，結果發現整個台灣都跟我一樣聰明。

en.content:
The biggest lesson of a summer trip:

the crowds you're trying to escape
all bought tickets
to the exact same place
on the exact same day as you.
en.editorNote: Timely for Taiwan's 2026 summer break (Jul 1–Aug 30) — works on the queue-at-a-hotspot photo, the packed-beach shot, or a summer-travel Threads lament. Nails the universal pain of peak-season crowds
variations: Summer travel taught me: the hidden gem you found is a secret shared by twenty-three million other people.
I deliberately avoided weekday traveling, only to discover the entire country was just as clever as me.

items.sourceNote: jet lag = body in two time zones frame，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
時差最奇妙的地方是：

我的身體在這裡，
我的腦子還在上一個城市，
我的胃
則自己決定現在是凌晨四點
該吃宵夜了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合回國第一週的黑眼圈自拍、半夜清醒滑手機的 Threads 廢發、IG 限動時差哀號——把時差那種「靈魂跟身體分居」的荒謬感寫得很有畫面
variations: 時差就是你的身體、腦子、跟胃，三個人各自過著不同的時區人生。
我沒有時差，我只是同時活在三個時區，而且每一個都在叫我去睡覺或吃東西。

en.content:
The strangest thing about jet lag is this:

my body is here,
my brain is still in the last city,
and my stomach
has unilaterally decided
it's 4 AM and time for a midnight snack.
en.editorNote: For the under-eye-circles first-week-back selfie, the wide-awake-at-3 AM Threads post, or the jet-lag IG story — captures the absurd 'body and soul living apart' feeling of time zones
variations: Jet lag is just your body, your brain, and your stomach each living in a different time zone.
I don't have jet lag. I'm just living in three time zones at once, and every one of them is telling me to sleep or eat.

items.sourceNote: 行李超重 / 23 公斤 register，原創

zh-tw.content:
託運行李上限二十三公斤，
是這個世界
對我的慾望
最溫柔也最殘酷的提醒。

二十二點八公斤，
剛剛好。
剩下零點二公斤的自制力，
我用一整趟旅程換來的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合機場磅秤特寫、行李炸開的平拍、Threads 出發前打包哀號——把「行李超重」 寫成一場跟自己慾望的拉鋸戰，社畜旅人共鳴度高
variations: 行李秤上那個數字，是我這個人最誠實的自畫像。
二十三公斤不是限制，是這個世界幫我設下、我自己卻一直想突破的底線。

en.content:
The 23-kilogram checked-bag limit
is the world's gentlest
and cruelest reminder
of how much I want.

22.8 kilos.
Just barely.
That last 0.2 kilos of self-control
cost me the entire trip.
en.editorNote: For the airport-scale close-up, the exploding-suitcase flat lay, or the pre-departure packing Threads post — turns overweight luggage into a tug-of-war with your own desires
variations: The number on the luggage scale is the most honest self-portrait I own.
23 kilos isn't a limit. It's the line the world drew for me that I keep trying to cross.

items.sourceNote: 貼文計畫 vs 收藏夾 register，原創

zh-tw.content:
我手機裡有一個資料夾，
叫做「總有一天要去」。

它已經
比我去過的所有地方
加起來還要大了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合收藏夾截圖、滿滿的待去清單照、Threads 旅行拖延自嘲——把「只收藏不出門」的現代通病寫成輕喜劇，誰的相簿沒有這個資料夾
variations: 我收藏的景點，比我這輩子實際踏上的還要遠、還要多、還要美。
「總有一天」 是我去過最多次的地方，可惜它不在地圖上。

en.content:
There's a folder on my phone
called 'someday.'

It's already bigger
than every place
I've actually been
combined.
en.editorNote: For the bookmark-folder screenshot, the overflowing-saved-list photo, or the travel-procrastination Threads post — turns the modern 'save it, never go' habit into a tiny comedy everyone's camera roll can relate to
variations: The places I've saved are farther, more numerous, and more beautiful than the ones I've actually set foot in.
'Someday' is the place I've visited most. Shame it isn't on any map.

items.sourceNote: 「I speak local time and tired」 jet lag bilingual frame，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
回國以後我變成雙語人：
白天我說當地時間，
晚上我說「累」。

兩種語言，
身體都聽不太懂。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合回國第三天還在凌晨清醒的 Threads 廢發、頂著黑眼圈上班的 IG 限動——把時差講成一種沒人教過的新語言，自帶笑點
variations: 我的時區不是亂掉，是同時開了兩個，互相搶我的睡眠。
別人回國帶伴手禮，我帶回一個壞掉的生理時鐘。

en.content:
After the trip I became bilingual:
by day I speak local time,
by night I speak tired.

My body understands
neither one.
en.editorNote: For the 4 AM-still-awake Threads post or the under-eye-circles back-at-work selfie — reframes jet lag as a language nobody taught you to speak
variations: My time zones aren't broken. There are just two of them now, fighting over my sleep.
Other people bring home souvenirs. I brought home a broken internal clock.

items.sourceNote: 「best company is your own, no one steals your fries」 solo travel frame，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
一個人旅行最大的好處，
不是自由，
不是自我成長，
是沒有人會
趁你不注意
偷吃你最後一根薯條。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合獨旅美食照、一個人的餐桌自拍、Threads 獨旅心得短發——把「獨旅好處」 從心靈雞湯拉回最真實的生活小確幸，反而更打動人
variations: 獨旅教會我最實用的一課：我的盤子，我說了算。
一個人吃飯不孤單，孤單的是有人陪你吃還跟你搶最後一塊。

en.content:
The best part of solo travel
isn't the freedom,
isn't the personal growth.

It's that nobody
reaches over
and steals your last fry.
en.editorNote: For the solo-meal flat lay, the table-for-one selfie, or the Threads solo-trip reflection — pulls the 'solo travel' cliché back down to the small, real joy that actually lands
variations: Solo travel's most practical lesson: my plate, my rules.
Eating alone isn't lonely. Lonely is having someone there who fights you for the last bite.

items.sourceNote: 「deodorant is a biohazard but emotional baggage clears customs」 airport security frame，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
機場安檢的邏輯我一直沒搞懂：
我的一瓶體香劑
被當成生化武器沒收，
但我整袋的情緒包袱
卻每次都順利過關。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合安檢排隊的 Threads 廢發、行李被翻開檢查的 IG 限動——把安檢的荒謬跟「情緒包袱」 雙關結合，誰過過海關都會心一笑
variations: 安檢可以掃出我牙膏超過 100 毫升，卻掃不出我心裡那些放不下的東西。
在機場，最難通關的從來不是行李，是我捨不得留在上一段旅程的心情。

en.content:
I've never understood airport security:
my one bottle of deodorant
gets confiscated as a biohazard,
but my entire bag of emotional baggage
clears customs every single time.
en.editorNote: For the security-line Threads post or the bag-getting-searched IG story — fuses the absurdity of screening with the emotional-baggage pun in a way every traveler has felt
variations: Security can flag my 100ml toothpaste but never the stuff I actually can't put down.
At the airport, the hardest thing to get through customs is the mood I left in the last city.

items.sourceNote: 「travel comes from a word meaning trouble, your delayed flight is on brand」 etymology frame，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
據說「旅行」這個詞
最早的意思
跟「折騰」很接近。

所以你的班機誤點四小時，
嚴格來說，
只是很忠於原意而已。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合誤點滯留機場的 Threads 哀號、登機門前苦笑自拍、班機資訊看板照——用冷知識把誤點的怒氣轉成段子，比直接抱怨高明
variations: 旅行的本質就是折騰，所以一切順利反而才是意外。
誤點不是意外，是旅行最古老的傳統，我們只是不小心傳承得太徹底。

en.content:
They say the word 'travel'
originally meant something close to 'trouble.'

So your four-hour delay
isn't bad luck.
It's just staying true
to the original meaning.
en.editorNote: For the stranded-at-the-gate Threads post, the wry boarding-area selfie, or the departure-board photo — uses a bit of etymology trivia to turn delay-rage into a punchline
variations: Travel was always meant to be trouble. So honestly, smooth trips are the real anomaly.
A delayed flight isn't a glitch. It's the oldest tradition in travel, and we're just honoring it too well.

items.sourceNote: layover / 轉機過夜 register，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
轉機等八小時，
我從一個觀光客，
慢慢變成了
這座機場的長期住戶。

登機門旁邊那排椅子，
現在我認得每一道刮痕。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合滯留轉機站的 Threads 哀號、半夜機場地板自拍、登機門排椅平拍——把漫長轉機的無聊放大成一種荒謬的歸屬感，比直接抱怨好笑
variations: 轉機八小時不叫旅行，叫在別人的國家罰站。
我跟這座機場的關係，已經從過客升級成熟客了。

en.content:
Eight-hour layover
and I've slowly gone
from a tourist
to a long-term resident of this airport.

I now recognize
every scratch on the gate-side chairs.
en.editorNote: For the stranded-at-connection Threads post, the floor-of-the-terminal selfie, or the gate-seating flat lay — blows up the boredom of a long layover into an absurd sense of belonging
variations: An eight-hour layover isn't travel. It's standing in the corner of someone else's country.
My relationship with this airport has officially upgraded from passing through to regular.

items.sourceNote: hotel breakfast buffet register，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
飯店早餐吃到飽
是少數幾個場合，
讓我可以在早上九點
理直氣壯地
同時拿三盤食物，
還假裝自己只是在試吃。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合飯店自助餐盤滿出來的平拍、早餐區可頌特寫、Threads 度假廢發——把人人都做過的「早餐拿太多」 寫成一種度假特權
variations: 飯店早餐的隱藏規則：吃不下也要拿，因為那是房價的一部分。
我度假的儀式感，就是把可以塞進胃裡的東西全部塞進那一盤。

en.content:
A hotel breakfast buffet
is one of the few places
where I can grab three plates
at nine in the morning,
fully unashamed,
and pretend I'm just sampling.
en.editorNote: For the overflowing-buffet-plate flat lay, the croissant close-up, or the lazy-vacation Threads post — reframes the universal 'I took too much' moment as a vacation entitlement
variations: The unwritten rule of hotel breakfast: take it even if you can't finish it, because it's baked into the room rate.
My vacation ritual is fitting everything my stomach can hold onto a single plate.

items.sourceNote: useless souvenir / 用不到的紀念品 register，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
我買的紀念品分成兩種：
一種是回國後
放進抽屜再也沒拿出來的，
另一種是
我連自己當初為什麼買
都想不起來的。

但我下次還是會買。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合戰利品開箱平拍、抽屜雜物特寫、Threads 回國盤點廢發——把「買沒用的東西」 講成一種戒不掉的旅行習慣，誰整理過行李都認同
variations: 紀念品的命運只有兩種：積灰塵，或被我送給也用不到的朋友。
我不是在買紀念品，我是在用一個鑰匙圈證明我真的去過那裡。

en.content:
My souvenirs come in two types:
the ones that go into a drawer
and never come back out,
and the ones
I can't even remember
buying.

I'll buy more next time anyway.
en.editorNote: For the souvenir-haul flat lay, the junk-drawer close-up, or the post-trip 'why did I buy this' Threads post — names the unbreakable habit of buying stuff you'll never use
variations: Souvenirs have two destinies: collecting dust, or getting handed to a friend who also won't use them.
I'm not buying a souvenir. I'm buying a keychain that proves I was actually there.

items.sourceNote: middle seat / 中間座位扶手 etiquette register，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
坐飛機中間座位的人，
是這個世界上
最被低估的外交官。

左邊一隻手肘、
右邊一隻手肘，
兩個扶手，
而我要在三萬五千英尺的高空
處理一場沒有贏家的領土談判。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合廉航窄座位自拍、登機證座位欄特寫、Threads 搶不到靠窗的哀號——把中間座位的扶手之爭升格成國際外交，自帶畫面跟笑點
variations: 中間座位教會我的事：有些戰爭，從你劃位的那一刻就註定要輸。
靠窗看風景，靠走道好上廁所，中間座位負責修身養性。

en.content:
Whoever sits in the middle seat
is the most underrated diplomat
in the world.

An elbow on the left,
an elbow on the right,
two armrests,
and me, at thirty-five thousand feet,
negotiating a border dispute with no winners.
en.editorNote: For the cramped-budget-airline selfie, the boarding-pass seat-assignment close-up, or the 'didn't get the window' Threads post — promotes the armrest war to international diplomacy
variations: The middle seat taught me that some wars are lost the moment you pick your seat.
Window seat is for the view, aisle is for the bathroom, and the middle seat is for building character.

items.sourceNote: airline change / 改簽手續費 register，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
我最放鬆的活動，
是跟航空公司客服
為了一筆
比機票還貴的改票手續費，
進行一場
語氣超有禮貌
但內心已經起火的對話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合改票改到崩潰的 Threads 廢發、盯著手機等客服的 IG 限動、機場櫃台前的苦笑自拍——把跟航空公司鬥智鬥勇的日常寫成黑色幽默
variations: 改一次票的手續費，夠我再飛一趟廉航，這就是現代魔術。
我不是在跟客服吵架，我是在用最客氣的語氣求他別再收我錢。

en.content:
My most relaxing activity
is a conversation with airline customer service
over a change fee
that costs more than the ticket itself —
a conversation where my tone stays polite
while my insides are quietly on fire.
en.editorNote: For the rebooking-meltdown Threads post, the on-hold-with-support IG story, or the wry airport-counter selfie — turns the daily battle with airlines into dark comedy
variations: One change fee costs enough for another budget flight. That's modern magic.
I'm not arguing with customer service. I'm politely begging them to stop charging me.

items.sourceNote: lost in translation / 翻譯 App 出包 register，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
我本來想用翻譯 App 點一碗湯，
結果店員笑了三秒，
端上來一盤我從沒看過的東西。

很好吃。
我到現在還是不知道那是什麼。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合異國小吃特寫、語言不通的餐廳合照、Threads 出糗分享——把「翻譯翻車」 寫成最有人情味的旅行意外
variations: 翻譯 App 最大的功能，是讓你跟陌生人一起對著手機螢幕笑出來。
在語言不通的地方點菜，本質上就是一場你跟運氣的合作。

en.content:
I tried to order soup with a translation app.
The waiter laughed for three seconds
and brought out something I'd never seen before.

It was delicious.
I still don't know what it was.
en.editorNote: For the mystery-dish close-up, the language-barrier restaurant photo, or the Threads 'point and pray' story — turns a translation fail into the warmest kind of travel accident
variations: The real feature of a translation app is making you and a total stranger laugh at the same phone screen.
Ordering food where you don't speak the language is basically a joint venture between you and luck.

items.sourceNote: 「I know where we're going / 五個錯誤轉彎前」 frame，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
「我知道路啦。」

——這句話，
是我五個錯誤轉彎以前說的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合巷弄迷路的廢照、跟旅伴鬥嘴後的 Threads 短發、走錯路四十分鐘後的苦笑自拍——一句話講完所有「自信導航」的下場
variations: 我手機有 GPS、有離線地圖、有導航 App，最後還是靠直覺把我們帶到死巷。
旅行中最危險的一句話不是「再走五分鐘就到」，是「相信我，我認得路」。

en.content:
"I know where we're going."

— me, five wrong turns ago.
en.editorNote: For the dead-end-alley photo, the post-bickering Threads quip, or the 40-minutes-the-wrong-way selfie — one line that captures every overconfident navigator's fate
variations: I have GPS, offline maps, and a navigation app, and somehow my gut still led us straight into a dead end.
The most dangerous sentence on a trip isn't 'just five more minutes.' It's 'trust me, I know the way.'

items.sourceNote: 「just here for the selfies / tourist trap」 register，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
我排了一小時的隊，
擠過三百個觀光客，
只為了拍一張
看起來像是
「現場只有我一個人」的照片。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合知名景點人擠人的真實照、網美打卡點排隊的 Threads 廢發、IG 美照後的爆料文——把「觀光客拍照文化」 自嘲到位又不刻薄
variations: 那張歲月靜好的照片，背後是三百個人的後腦勺跟我快斷掉的耐心。
我不是來感受文化的，我是來跟另外三百個人搶同一個拍照角度的。

en.content:
I waited in line for an hour,
elbowed past three hundred tourists,
all to take one photo
that looks like
I had the whole place to myself.
en.editorNote: For the actually-crowded landmark reality shot, the queue-at-the-photo-spot Threads post, or the honest follow-up to a glossy IG pic — self-deprecating about tourist culture without being mean
variations: That serene, peaceful photo? It's standing on three hundred other people's heads and my last shred of patience.
I'm not here to soak in the culture. I'm here to fight three hundred people for the same camera angle.

items.sourceNote: 「airports: where sleep and time don't exist」 register，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
在機場，
時間是不存在的。

所以早上七點喝啤酒合理、
下午三點睡地板合理、
一包洋芋片賣你一百八
也，勉強，合理。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合轉機過夜的崩潰自拍、登機門前躺平的 Threads 廢發、機場美食街天價收據照——把機場那種時空失序的荒謬感寫到位
variations: 機場是地表唯一一個你睡在地板上，還會有人羨慕你終於找到位子的地方。
在機場待超過六小時，你的生理時鐘會主動辭職。

en.content:
At the airport,
time doesn't exist.

So a 7 AM beer is fine,
a 3 PM nap on the floor is fine,
and a bag of chips for nine dollars
is, somehow, also fine.
en.editorNote: For the overnight-layover meltdown selfie, the sprawled-at-the-gate Threads post, or the outrageous-airport-receipt photo — nails the time-warp absurdity every traveler has lived
variations: The airport is the only place on earth where people envy you for finally finding a spot on the floor to sleep.
Spend more than six hours in an airport and your body clock quietly hands in its resignation.

items.sourceNote: sincere reflective register × 回憶 frame，原創

zh-tw.content:
你不會記得那天的天氣，
不會記得門票多少錢，
但你會記得
那個轉角突然出現的海，
以及當下
你毫無防備地笑出來的自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合旅行回顧長文、年末總結、把舊照片翻出來的 IG caption——把「旅行為什麼值得」 講得很溫柔、不喊口號
variations: 旅行帶不走的東西很多，但帶得走的，剛好都是最重要的那些。
多年以後，你忘了行程表上的每一個景點，卻記得某個不在計畫裡的瞬間。

en.content:
You won't remember the weather that day,
or how much the ticket cost.

But you'll remember
the ocean that appeared around the corner,
and the version of you
that laughed before you could stop it.
en.editorNote: For the year-end travel recap, the reflective long caption, or the old-photo throwback post — explains why travel is worth it, gently and without slogans
variations: Travel leaves a lot behind. But what it lets you keep happens to be the part that matters most.
Years later, you'll forget every stop on the itinerary and remember the one moment that wasn't on it.

items.sourceNote: Airbnb checkout chore list register，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
飯店退房：把房卡丟進盒子就好。

民宿退房：
洗碗、收垃圾、把床單拆下來、
關掉七個開關、
還要拍照證明你沒偷走那株多肉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合民宿廚房收尾的 IG 限動、退房前手忙腳亂的 Threads 短發、跟朋友吐槽訂房落差——把飯店與民宿的退房差距講得超有共鳴
variations: 我付錢來度假，最後在退房前完成了一場大掃除，這趟到底誰是房客。
民宿主人留的退房須知比我公司的離職交接還詳細。

en.content:
Hotel checkout:
drop the keycard in a box.

Airbnb checkout:
wash the dishes, take out the trash,
strip the sheets, flip seven switches,
and photograph proof
you didn't steal the succulent.
en.editorNote: For the Airbnb-kitchen-cleanup story, the frantic pre-checkout Threads post, or the 'who's the guest here' rant — nails the gap between hotel ease and rental chores
variations: I paid to relax and ended up doing a full house clean before checkout. Remind me who the guest was.
The host's checkout instructions were more detailed than my company's offboarding paperwork.

items.sourceNote: flight delay limbo register，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
班機延誤的時候，
登機門前的廣播
就像渣男的「我快到了」：
你知道它在說謊，
但你還是抱著一點希望坐在那裡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合延誤滯留登機門的自拍、跑馬燈一直跳延誤的 Threads 廢發、機場地板坐到天荒地老的 IG 限動——把延誤的無奈寫成段子
variations: 延誤兩小時，航空公司請我吃了一張二十塊的餐券，我們的關係就靠這張券維繫著。
登機資訊牌上的『預計起飛時間』，跟我的新年計畫一樣，純屬參考。

en.content:
A delayed flight
is the airport announcing
like a guy who texts 'almost there':
you know it's lying,
but you keep sitting there hoping anyway.
en.editorNote: For the stranded-at-the-gate selfie, the ever-updating delay-board Threads post, or the floor-sitting limbo story — turns the helplessness of a delay into a punchline
variations: Two-hour delay. The airline gave me a $20 meal voucher. That voucher is the only thing holding this relationship together.
The 'estimated departure time' on the board is exactly as binding as my New Year's resolutions.

items.sourceNote: 「sunscreen I forgot」 sunburn tourist truth register，原創

zh-tw.content:
我帶了防曬乳，
我擦了防曬乳，
我每兩小時補一次防曬乳。

結果還是曬成一隻不會游泳的龍蝦。

太陽，我們本來可以好好相處的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合海島度假回來脫皮的 IG 限動、紅通通自拍、Threads 抱怨文——把每個人都中過的曬傷悲劇寫成自嘲喜劇
variations: 回國最明顯的紀念品，是身上那條穿背心曬出來的分界線。
別人帶回古銅色小麥肌，我帶回一身會痛的粉紅色跟脫皮的後悔。

en.content:
I packed the sunscreen.
I applied the sunscreen.
I reapplied every two hours.

And I still came home
as a lobster that can't swim.

Sun, we could've worked this out.
en.editorNote: For the post-island peeling-skin story, the bright-red selfie, or the Threads complaint thread — turns the universal sunburn tragedy into self-aware comedy
variations: My most obvious souvenir is the tan line shaped exactly like the tank top I wore.
Other people came back golden and glowing. I came back pink, painful, and full of regret.

items.sourceNote: Jarod Kintz time-zone immortality frame，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
我往西飛了一趟，
落地的時候比出發還早，
所以技術上來說，
我剛剛變年輕了。

如果我一直往西飛，
是不是就能逃過下週一的早會？
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合跨時區長途飛行的 IG 限動、紅眼班機 Threads 廢發、收假前一晚的厭世發文——把時區的荒謬感玩成腦洞段子
variations: 我搭飛機賺到了五個小時，全部拿去補出發前那晚沒睡的覺，收支剛好打平。
時區是世界上唯一可以合法讓你回到過去的東西，可惜只能用來補眠。

en.content:
I flew west,
landed earlier than I left,
so technically
I just got younger.

If I keep flying west,
can I outrun Monday's standup meeting?
en.editorNote: For the long-haul time-zone story, the red-eye Threads post, or the Sunday-night dread caption — spins the absurdity of time zones into a brain-bending bit
variations: I gained five hours on this flight and spent every one of them recovering from the night before. We broke even.
Time zones are the only legal way to travel back in time, and the best you can do with it is nap.

items.sourceNote: 「here for the continental breakfast」 hotel buffet register，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
你問我這間飯店值不值得？

我不知道，
我整趟的記憶
只剩早餐吧那台
可以無限取用的優格機
跟現烤可頌。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合飯店自助早餐戰利品平拍、可頌特寫、Threads 美食廢發——把住飯店最被低估的快樂（吃到飽早餐）講得理直氣壯
variations: 我訂飯店的第一個考量不是房間，是早餐有沒有現煎蛋區。
判斷一間飯店好不好，看退房那天我願不願意為了早餐早起就知道了。

en.content:
You ask if the hotel was worth it?

I couldn't tell you.
My entire memory of the trip
is the unlimited yogurt machine
and the fresh croissants
at the breakfast bar.
en.editorNote: For the loaded-breakfast-plate flat lay, the croissant close-up, or the foodie Threads post — owns the most underrated hotel joy with zero shame
variations: The first thing I check when booking a hotel isn't the room. It's whether there's a make-your-own-omelet station.
You can judge any hotel by one question: was I willing to wake up early for the breakfast?

items.sourceNote: 「carry-on queen until souvenirs」 register，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
我出發時是登機箱界的極簡王者，
回程時是
託運超重 700 塊、
手上還拎兩個紙袋的
紀念品奴隸。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合戰利品爆滿的行李平拍、機場託運櫃台前的 Threads 哀號、回國開箱文——把「去輕回重」 的旅人宿命寫得很有畫面
variations: 我發誓這次只帶登機箱，然後我在第三天遇到了一間我管不住自己的雜貨店。
出國前的我跟回國後的我，差了整整一個託運行李的重量。

en.content:
I left as a carry-on minimalist royal.
I'm coming home
as a souvenir slave
with a $40 overweight fee
and two paper bags in each hand.
en.editorNote: For the overflowing-suitcase flat lay, the check-in-counter Threads lament, or the souvenir-haul unboxing — captures the universal 'light there, heavy back' fate
variations: I swore I'd do carry-on only. Then on day three I met a gift shop I had no power over.
The me who left and the me who came back are exactly one checked bag apart.

items.sourceNote: 「wanted adventure, turned out I wanted snacks」 frame，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
我以為我要的是冒險，
後來發現
我要的只是
在陌生的便利商店
找到一個沒看過的零食口味。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合異國超商貨架照、零食戰利品平拍、Threads 旅行廢發——把旅人最真實的小確幸（逛當地超商）寫到位，誰看了都笑著點頭
variations: 別人出國爬山看極光，我出國的最高成就是在當地超商買到限定口味洋芋片。
我的旅行重點不是景點，是這個國家的便利商店有沒有藏著驚喜。

en.content:
I thought I wanted adventure.
Turns out
what I wanted
was a flavor of chips
I'd never seen before
in a foreign convenience store.
en.editorNote: For the foreign-supermarket-shelf photo, the snack-haul flat lay, or the lighthearted travel Threads post — nails the quiet joy every traveler secretly chases
variations: Some people travel for the northern lights. My peak achievement was a limited-edition chip flavor at a local 7-Eleven.
My itinerary isn't about landmarks. It's about whether this country's convenience store is hiding something good.

items.sourceNote: 「airplane mode」 雙關 frame，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
登機之後，
我把手機調成飛航模式，
順便把自己
也調成飛航模式：
不回訊息、不接電話、
不處理任何人的人生。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合登機前最後一則限動、機艙窗景照、年假爆發出國的 Threads 短發——把「飛航模式」 從手機設定升級成一種人生狀態
variations: 起飛那一刻最爽的不是離地，是我終於有正當理由已讀不回全世界。
飛航模式開啟中，本人暫時離線，煩惱請改日再來。

en.content:
Once I board,
I switch my phone to airplane mode
and quietly switch myself to it too:
no replies, no calls,
no handling anyone else's life
for the next eleven hours.
en.editorNote: For the last-story-before-boarding, the cabin-window shot, or the 'using all my PTO' Threads post — upgrades airplane mode from a phone setting to a state of being
variations: The best part of takeoff isn't leaving the ground. It's finally having a valid excuse to leave the whole world on read.
Airplane mode: on. Me: temporarily offline. Problems: please try again later.

items.sourceNote: 「jet lag is the price of travel」 Sarah Dessen frame，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
時差是旅行附贈的帳單，
你以為飛機票已經付清了，
結果回國第五天
凌晨三點清醒地盯著天花板，
你才知道
還有尾款要分期繳。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合回國凌晨失眠的 Threads 廢發、頂著黑眼圈上班的 IG 限動——把時差講成一筆「分期付款」，比直接抱怨好笑也好用
variations: 旅行的快樂是一次付清的，時差是後面慢慢扣的循環利息。
我以為回到家旅行就結束了，沒想到時差還要再陪我加演一週。

en.content:
Jet lag is the bill travel adds on.
You thought the plane ticket settled it,
but on day five back home,
wide awake at 3 AM
staring at the ceiling,
you realize there's a balance due in installments.
en.editorNote: For the back-home insomnia Threads post or the under-eye-circles desk selfie — frames jet lag as a payment plan, funnier and truer than a plain complaint
variations: The joy of travel is paid up front. Jet lag is the interest it quietly charges later.
I thought the trip ended when I got home. Jet lag booked itself a one-week encore.

items.sourceNote: 「friends trip = memories and one argument」 frame，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
跟朋友出國一定會帶回兩樣東西：
一輩子忘不了的回憶，
跟一場
關於「到底是誰說要走這條路」
的世紀爭吵。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合旅伴合照、迷路現場自拍、回國後揭瘡疤的 Threads 短發——把團體旅行的友情真相寫得又準又好笑，誰揪團出去都中槍
variations: 好的旅伴不是不會吵架，是吵完還能一起去吃下一攤。
團體旅行的回憶有一半是風景，另一半是那天我們在路口僵持的十五分鐘。

en.content:
A trip with friends always brings back two things:
memories you'll keep forever,
and one legendary argument
about who exactly
said to take this road.
en.editorNote: For the travel-squad group shot, the lost-at-the-crossroads selfie, or the post-trip 'let me explain' Threads post — nails the truth of group travel, hilarious and accurate
variations: A good travel buddy isn't one who never fights with you. It's one who'll still go grab the next meal after.
Half my group-trip memories are the scenery. The other half is the fifteen minutes we stood frozen at that intersection.

items.sourceNote: 「emotional baggage / suitcase drama queen」 frame，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
我的行李箱是個戲精，
平常安安靜靜，
一上磅秤就開始演
「我這輩子都沒這麼重過」。

超重的不是它，
是我捨不得丟的那些「萬一用得到」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合 check-in 櫃台磅秤照、行李炸開平拍、Threads 出國前廢發——把行李超重的尷尬怪到行李身上，比直接認帳好笑也好用
variations: 行李箱在家很乖，到了磅秤前就開始耍賴，這點跟我很像。
超重費不是罰金，是我的優柔寡斷被換算成現金的結果。

en.content:
My suitcase is a drama queen.
Quiet all year,
then the second it hits the scale
it starts performing
'I have never been this heavy in my life.'

It's not the bag that's overweight.
It's every 'just in case' I couldn't leave behind.
en.editorNote: For the check-in-scale photo, the exploded-suitcase flat lay, or the night-before-trip Threads post — blames the overweight bag on the suitcase instead of yourself, funnier than just confessing
variations: My suitcase behaves at home and throws a tantrum the moment it sees the scale. Honestly, same.
The overweight fee isn't a fine. It's my indecision converted into cash.

items.sourceNote: 「layover so long I'll start a new life」 frame，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
這個轉機等待時間長到，
我已經認識了登機門旁邊的店員、
背熟了廣播的順序、
還默默替隔壁睡著的旅客
想好了他的人生故事。

再等下去，
我大概要在這裡落地生根了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合漫長轉機的 Threads 哀號、機場候機區自拍、紅眼班機 IG 限動——把「轉機地獄」 寫成黑色幽默，誰被卡過機場都懂
variations: 轉機八小時，我從旅客變成這座航廈的常住人口。
別人的轉機是過渡，我的轉機是一段完整的人生體驗。

en.content:
This layover is so long
I've befriended the gate-side barista,
memorized the order of the announcements,
and quietly written
a full backstory
for the stranger asleep next to me.

If I wait any longer
I'm putting down roots here.
en.editorNote: For the endless-layover Threads complaint, the departure-lounge selfie, or the red-eye IG story — turns layover purgatory into deadpan comedy every stranded traveler gets
variations: Eight-hour layover. I came in as a passenger and I'm leaving as a permanent resident of this terminal.
For other people a layover is a transit. For me it's becoming a full chapter of my life.

items.sourceNote: 「collect moments not things」 經典 frame，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
他們說「收集回憶，不要收集東西」。

我兩個都收集，
因為十年後
我會忘記那天的天氣，
但我會記得
是哪張車票
帶我去看了那場日落。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合車票明信片平拍、抽屜裡的旅行小物特寫、年末回顧文——把「收集回憶」 老梗加一層真實，比純文青口號更打動人
variations: 回憶會褪色，但那張皺皺的票根會幫我把它撿回來。
我不是囤積狂，我只是替未來健忘的自己留證據。

en.content:
They say 'collect moments, not things.'

I collect both,
because in ten years
I'll forget what the weather was like,
but I'll remember
exactly which ticket stub
took me to that sunset.
en.editorNote: For the ticket-stub-and-postcard flat lay, the drawer-of-travel-keepsakes close-up, or the year-end reflection post — adds an honest twist to a worn wanderlust cliché
variations: Memories fade. A crumpled ticket stub helps me reach back and grab them again.
I'm not a hoarder. I'm just leaving evidence for the forgetful version of me down the road.

items.sourceNote: 「airport coffee costs an arm and a leg」 frame，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
機場的咖啡賣三百塊，
但我還是買了。

不是因為好喝，
是因為那一刻
它是唯一一個
沒有在催我登機、
沒有在問我護照、
願意安靜陪我五分鐘的東西。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合機場咖啡杯特寫、候機區疲憊自拍、Threads 出國前廢發——把「機場物價搶錢」 翻成一種荒謬的溫柔，自帶笑點又有共鳴
variations: 我不是被機場咖啡搶錢，是花三百塊買一個沒人催我的早晨。
機場的咖啡很貴，但它是少數不會對我廣播「最後登機通知」的存在。

en.content:
The airport coffee costs nine dollars,
and I bought it anyway.

Not because it's good,
but because in that moment
it was the only thing
not rushing me to board,
not asking for my passport,
willing to just sit with me for five quiet minutes.
en.editorNote: For the airport-coffee-cup close-up, the tired-gate-area selfie, or the pre-flight Threads post — flips the 'airport prices are a scam' gripe into something absurdly tender and relatable
variations: I'm not getting robbed by airport coffee. I'm paying nine dollars for a morning nobody is rushing me through.
The coffee's overpriced, but it's the only thing in this terminal that won't page me for final boarding.

items.sourceNote: 「heart says book the flight, bank account says no」 經典 frame，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
我的存款說：不行。
我的心說：訂下去。

我的滑鼠
聽我的心。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合衝動訂票後的截圖、刷卡瞬間的 IG 限動、Threads 自我合理化短發——把每個人都犯過的「理智輸給機票」 寫成三幕劇
variations: 理智跟我說省一點，機票特價跟我說機會難得，最後是特價贏了。
錢包在求我冷靜，我的手指已經按下確認付款。

en.content:
My bank account says no.
My heart says book it.

My mouse
listens to my heart.
en.editorNote: For the impulse-booking screenshot, the card-in-hand IG story, or the 'I have no self-control' Threads post — the universal three-act tragedy of every traveler
variations: My brain said save money. The flight sale said now or never. The sale won.
My wallet begged me to be reasonable. My finger had already hit 'confirm payment.'

items.sourceNote: 「happy place with no Wi-Fi」 register，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
我找到我的快樂天堂了，
那裡沒有訊號。

剛開始我很恐慌，
第三天我終於想起來
原來世界沒有我也轉得很好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合無訊號山區、離島、深山民宿的 IG caption、數位排毒 Threads 短發——把「沒網路」 從災難寫成療癒，多一層真實的心境轉折
variations: 沒訊號的第一天像戒斷，第三天像重生。
這裡收不到訊號，所以我終於收得到自己。

en.content:
I found my happy place.
It has no Wi-Fi.

Day one I panicked.
By day three I remembered
the world spins just fine without me.
en.editorNote: For the off-grid mountain shot, the remote-island caption, or the digital-detox Threads post — turns 'no signal' from a disaster into a quiet relief, with one honest emotional turn
variations: Day one with no signal felt like withdrawal. Day three felt like being reborn.
There's no reception here, which is the only reason I finally heard myself.

items.sourceNote: 2026 set-jetting 影劇景點旅行 trend，原創

zh-tw.content:
我這趟不是去旅行，
是去確認
那部劇裡的場景
沒有開美顏濾鏡。

結果現場的風
比演員還會演。
zh-tw.editorNote: 2026 set-jetting（跟著影劇去旅行） 最 timely 的 caption——適合追劇朝聖打卡照、Threads 旅行心得、IG 對比劇照的限動
variations: 別人看劇看劇情，我看劇看可以飛去哪裡。
我的旅遊清單，其實是我的追劇清單長腳走出來的。

en.content:
I didn't come here to travel.
I came to check
whether the scene from the show
had a beauty filter on.

Turns out the real wind
acts better than the cast.
en.editorNote: Timely caption for 2026's set-jetting boom — pairs with a filming-location pilgrimage shot, a side-by-side with the show still, or a Threads post about binge-watching your way into a flight booking
variations: Other people watch a show for the plot. I watch it for the flight I'm about to book.
My travel bucket list is just my watchlist that grew legs and walked off the couch.

items.sourceNote: 2026 noctourism 夜遊 trend，原創

zh-tw.content:
白天的城市
是給觀光客看的。

凌晨兩點的城市
才願意小聲告訴你
它真正的樣子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 2026 noctourism（夜遊旅行）趨勢專屬——適合夜市、夜景、深夜街拍的 IG caption，把「半夜還在外面晃」 寫得很有氣氛、不像在虛度
variations: 我不是夜貓子，我是專程來收集這座城市卸妝後的樣子。
最美的旅行，常常發生在地圖打烊以後。

en.content:
The city in daylight
is the one they show tourists.

The city at 2 AM
is the one that finally whispers
what it's actually like.
en.editorNote: For 2026's noctourism trend — pairs with night-market shots, after-dark cityscapes, or the late-night street-photography caption. Reframes 'still out at 2 AM' as intentional, not aimless
variations: I'm not a night owl. I came to see this city with its makeup off.
The best part of a trip usually happens after the map closes for the night.

items.sourceNote: chronocation / 跟著生理時鐘旅行 trend，原創廢文

zh-tw.content:
現在流行一種旅行方式，
叫做「跟著自己的生理時鐘走」。

聽起來很高級，
其實就是
睡到自然醒、
餓了才吃、
不爽就回飯店。

我已經這樣活很多年了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 2026「chronocation（生理時鐘旅行）」 趨勢的台味吐槽——適合睡到中午才出門的 Threads 廢發、飯店賴床自拍、IG 自嘲文，把高級趨勢翻譯成廢人日常
variations: 別人排行程精準到分鐘，我的行程只有一條：身體說了算。
所謂的慢旅行，就是我把懶散包裝成一種生活哲學。

en.content:
There's a trendy new way to travel
called 'following your own body clock.'

Sounds very sophisticated.
It's really just
waking up whenever,
eating when hungry,
and going back to the hotel when annoyed.

I've been doing this for years.
en.editorNote: A deadpan take on 2026's chronocation trend — pairs with the slept-till-noon Threads post, the lazy-hotel-bed selfie, or any 'I am the slow travel movement' caption. Translates a fancy trend into glorious laziness
variations: Some people schedule their trips to the minute. My only rule is: whatever my body says, goes.
Slow travel is just me repackaging being lazy as a life philosophy.

items.sourceNote: 2026 台灣暑假 7/1-8/30 共 61 天 timely register，原創

zh-tw.content:
學生的暑假有 61 天，
上班族的暑假
是把特休拆成
三段週五加週一的拼貼藝術。

但我們玩得，
一點都不輸。
zh-tw.editorNote: 2026 暑假 7/1–8/30 主場期最 timely 的在地廢文——適合搶連假出遊的 IG caption、Threads 排假心得、跟學生朋友互嗆的 LINE 訊息；把上班族「沒有暑假」 的痛轉成驕傲
variations: 我沒有暑假，我只有用特休精算出來的偽暑假，而且比真的還珍貴。
學生放兩個月，我放兩天半，但我的兩天半含金量超高。

en.content:
Students get a 61-day summer break.
Office workers get summer
as a collage
of three long weekends
stitched together from saved-up PTO.

And honestly?
We vacation just as hard.
en.editorNote: For the long-weekend-getaway IG caption, the PTO-planning Threads post, or the back-to-office selfie — flips the office worker's 'no real summer' lament into quiet pride
variations: I don't get a summer break. I get a forged one, calculated down to the last PTO hour, and it's worth more.
Students get two months. I get two and a half days. But mine are pure gold.

items.sourceNote: summer heat / 防曬 register，原創

zh-tw.content:
夏天去海邊的真相：
防曬乳補了五次、
還是曬出一條安全帶痕、
IG 照片裡笑得燦爛，
回家洗澡才哭出來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合海邊比基尼照、曬傷後的鏡子自拍、Threads 夏日廢發——把海邊美照背後的曬傷狼狽寫得很真實，誰夏天去過海邊都中槍
variations: 別人去海邊收集回憶，我去海邊收集曬傷的層次感。
夏天最公平的事：再貴的防曬乳，都擋不住我對陽光的低估。

en.content:
The truth about the beach in summer:
you reapply sunscreen five times,
still end up with a seatbelt-shaped tan line,
smile beautifully in the photos,
and cry in the shower later.
en.editorNote: For the bikini beach shot, the post-sunburn mirror selfie, or the summer Threads post — captures the painful reality behind every glowing beach photo
variations: Other people go to the beach to collect memories. I go to collect layers of sunburn.
Summer's only fair rule: no sunscreen is strong enough to protect you from underestimating the sun.

items.sourceNote: 2026 旅展 / 機票促銷搶票 timely register，原創

zh-tw.content:
搶廉航特價機票的我，
手速比上班回訊息快十倍，
眼神比談戀愛還專注，
結帳前一秒
還是被加價的行李費
拉回現實。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合搶票成功截圖、旅展現場排隊照、Threads 半夜衝動訂票後的反省文——精準描寫每個搶廉航特價的人最熟悉的興奮與背刺
variations: 機票本身很便宜，貴的是它後面默默排隊的：行李、選位、餐點、跟我的衝動。
搶到特價機票的快樂，撐不過看到總金額的那一秒。

en.content:
Booking a budget flight deal,
my reflexes are ten times faster
than replying to work emails,
my focus sharper than any relationship —
then one second before checkout
the baggage fee
drags me back to reality.
en.editorNote: For the deal-snagged screenshot, the travel-fair queue photo, or the 2 AM impulse-booking Threads confession — captures the thrill and betrayal every budget-flight hunter knows
variations: The flight is cheap. What's expensive is everything quietly lining up behind it: bags, seats, meals, and my impulse.
The joy of a flight deal lasts exactly until the total appears.

items.sourceNote: summer trip / 暑假計畫 vs 現實 register，原創

zh-tw.content:
我夏天的旅遊計畫：
看遍三座島、
爬一座山、
讀完五本書。

我夏天的真實行程：
冷氣房裡躺平，
手機相簿翻去年的海，
計畫明年再說。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合暑假還沒出門就先躺平的 Threads 廢發、冷氣房自拍、IG 自嘲文——把每個人「計畫很豐滿、行動很骨感」 的暑假寫到極致
variations: 我的暑假待辦清單，每年都原封不動地延期到明年暑假。
計畫環島的我，跟躺在沙發上的我，是兩個從來沒見過面的人。

en.content:
My summer travel plans:
see three islands,
climb one mountain,
finish five books.

My actual summer:
lying flat in the AC,
scrolling last year's beach photos,
rescheduling everything to next year.
en.editorNote: For the haven't-left-the-couch Threads post, the air-conditioned-room selfie, or the self-deprecating IG caption — nails the eternal gap between summer ambition and summer reality
variations: My summer to-do list rolls over, untouched, to next summer. Every single year.
The me who plans an island-hopping trip and the me lying on the couch have never actually met.

items.sourceNote: flight delay / 班機延誤 register，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
我的班機沒有延誤，
它只是比較有個性，
想讓登機門前的我
好好練習一個現代人最稀缺的技能：
什麼都做不了，
還要假裝很平靜。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合誤點時的登機門廢照、Threads 哀號短發、IG 限動配一杯機場貴鬆鬆咖啡——把延誤的無力感寫成段子，比直接罵航空公司有梗
variations: 延誤三小時教我的事：耐心不是天生的，是被登機門逼出來的。
班機延誤的時候，連時間都在偷懶，憑什麼要我焦慮。

en.content:
My flight isn't delayed.
It's just fashionably late,
giving me a chance to practice
the rarest modern skill there is:
being completely unable to do anything
while pretending to be calm.
en.editorNote: For the slumped-at-the-gate selfie, the overpriced-airport-coffee flat lay, or the Threads delay rant — turns powerless gate-waiting into a punchline instead of an airline complaint
variations: A three-hour delay taught me patience isn't a virtue. It's a hostage situation at gate 22.
When the flight's delayed, even time is slacking off. So why am I the one panicking?

items.sourceNote: 「sorry for what I said when I wasn't on vacation」 經典 frame，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
為我放假前說過的每一句話道歉。

那不是我，
那是一個連續上班十一個月、
沒看過海、
忘記自己會笑的生物。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合放假第一天終於放鬆的海邊自拍、Threads 收心操短發、IG 度假限動——把「度假前後判若兩人」 的反差寫到極致，上班族秒共鳴
variations: 我度假前的脾氣，請大家當作是還沒解凍的我，不予採信。
放假第一天我才想起來，原來我是個還算好相處的人。

en.content:
Sorry for everything I said
before my vacation.

That wasn't me.
That was a creature
who'd worked eleven months straight,
hadn't seen the ocean,
and forgot it knew how to smile.
en.editorNote: For the day-one-of-vacation beach selfie, the finally-decompressing Threads post, or the resort IG story — leans into the before-and-after personality swap every office worker lives
variations: Please disregard my pre-vacation temper. That version of me hadn't thawed out yet.
Day one of vacation and I just remembered I'm actually a pretty pleasant person.

items.sourceNote: layover / 轉機 register，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
轉機八小時不是一段空檔，
是航空公司送你的
一個沒人認識你、
沒有 Wi-Fi、
物價是平常三倍的
平行世界體驗包。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合轉機癱在候機椅的廢照、Threads 半夜轉機機場短發、IG 限動配登機證——把又長又無聊的轉機寫成荒謬小宇宙，誰轉過機都懂
variations: 轉機的航廈是地表上唯一一個你可以躺在地上睡，還不會有人多看一眼的地方。
八小時的轉機讓我認清：我跟那張候機椅的感情，比有些前任還深。

en.content:
An eight-hour layover isn't dead time.
It's a starter pack
the airline hands you
for a parallel universe
where nobody knows your name,
there's no Wi-Fi,
and everything costs triple.
en.editorNote: For the sprawled-across-the-waiting-chairs photo, the 3 AM layover Threads post, or the boarding-pass-and-defeat IG story — turns the soul-crushing layover into an absurd little cosmos
variations: A transit terminal is the only place on earth you can sleep on the floor and nobody bats an eye.
Eight hours of layover and I've bonded with this waiting-chair harder than some of my exes.

items.sourceNote: summer heat / 夏天太熱不想出門 register，原創

zh-tw.content:
夏天的旅行有兩種：
一種是追著海跑，
一種是追著冷氣跑。

今年我選擇後者，
景點就是
從這間有冷氣的店
移動到下一間有冷氣的店。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在便利商店吹冷氣的廢照、夏天熱到融化的 Threads 短發、IG 自嘲限動——把台灣夏天「景點之間靠冷氣續命」 的真實寫到位，超在地
variations: 我夏天的行程規劃，是以「兩間冷氣之間的步行距離」 為單位計算的。
別人夏天收集海灘，我收集每一間我躲過太陽的便利商店。

en.content:
There are two kinds of summer trips:
one chases the ocean,
one chases the air conditioning.

This year I picked the second.
My sightseeing
is just moving
from one cooled room
to the next.
en.editorNote: For the hiding-in-the-convenience-store selfie, the melting-in-the-heat Threads post, or the self-aware summer IG story — captures the very real subtropical strategy of surviving by AC hops
variations: I plan my summer itinerary in one unit: walking distance between two air conditioners.
Other people collect beaches in summer. I collect every shop I ducked into to escape the sun.

items.sourceNote: 2026 anti-curation / 真實感取代完美 trend register，原創

zh-tw.content:
今年我決定不再為了一張美照
重拍二十次。

糊掉的、晃到的、
半張臉被風吹歪的，
才是我那天真的在場的證據。
zh-tw.editorNote: 呼應 2026 社群「去精修、要真實」 的轉向——適合放失敗照合輯、Threads 反濾鏡短發、IG 隨手拍九宮格，比起完美擺拍更有故事感
variations: 最好的旅行照不是最美的那張，是你按下快門時根本沒在管美不美的那張。
我相簿裡那些手震、過曝、亂入路人的照片，比任何精修圖都更像那一天。

en.content:
This year I'm done
reshooting the same photo twenty times
for one perfect frame.

The blurry ones,
the shaky ones,
the half-my-face-blown-sideways ones —
those are the proof
I was actually there.
en.editorNote: Rides the 2026 shift away from over-curated feeds toward 'the vibe' — for the imperfect photo dump, the anti-filter Threads post, or the candid IG carousel where the messy shots tell the real story
variations: The best travel photo isn't the prettiest one. It's the one you took without caring whether it was pretty.
The blurry, overexposed, stranger-photobombed shots in my camera roll look more like that day than any edited one.
